title: Chili Mac with Beans
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no cholesterol-contributing animal-derived ingredients. The kidney beans, pinto beans, macaroni, vegetables, olive oil, tomatoes, tomato paste, vegetable stock, herbs, spices, vinegar, lime juice, and fresh herbs contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the recipe is built around dried beans, a legume base rich in soluble fiber, with vegetables, tomato, pasta, aromatics, spices, and unsaturated oil providing structure and flavor without cholesterol-containing ingredients.
intro: Chili mac is American cafeteria, school-lunch, potluck, and weeknight comfort food: chili and macaroni brought together in one pot until the pasta, beans, tomato, and spices become one big spoonable meal. It lives somewhere between chili, goulash, and baked pasta, depending on the region and the kitchen. The best version should be hearty, tomato-rich, a little smoky, and satisfying without turning into glue. Beans give it substance, macaroni gives it comfort, and the sauce has to stay loose enough to coat everything.

stepGroups.title: Soak the Beans
items: Place the kidney beans ({{quantity:dried-kidney-beans}}) and pinto beans ({{quantity:dried-pinto-beans}}) in a large bowl and cover with several inches of water.
Soak overnight.
Drain and rinse the beans.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Beans
items: Add the soaked beans, water ({{quantity:water-cooking-beans}}), and bay leaf ({{quantity:bay-leaf}}) to a pot.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Cook for 60 to 90 minutes, until the beans are tender but not falling apart.
Drain the beans and discard the bay leaf.

stepGroups.title: Build the Chili Base
items: Heat the olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil}}) in a large Dutch oven or heavy pot over medium heat.
Add the onion ({{quantity:yellow-onion}}), green bell pepper ({{quantity:green-bell-pepper}}), and red bell pepper ({{quantity:red-bell-pepper}}).
Cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring often, until the vegetables soften and the onion turns translucent.

Add the garlic ({{quantity:garlic}}), chili powder ({{quantity:chili-powder}}), cumin ({{quantity:ground-cumin}}), smoked paprika ({{quantity:smoked-paprika}}), oregano ({{quantity:dried-oregano}}), black pepper ({{quantity:black-pepper}}), and cayenne ({{quantity:cayenne-pepper}}).
Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until the spices smell toasted and the garlic is fragrant.

stepGroups.title: Build the Sauce
items: Add the tomato paste ({{quantity:tomato-paste}}).
Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until it darkens slightly and starts to stick to the bottom of the pot.
Add the crushed tomatoes ({{quantity:crushed-tomatoes}}), vegetable stock ({{quantity:vegetable-stock}}), fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt}}), and cooked beans.
Bring to a simmer.
Cook uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chili base thickens slightly and the beans absorb some of the seasoning.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Macaroni
items: Bring a separate pot of water to a boil.
Cook the elbow macaroni ({{quantity:elbow-macaroni}}) until 2 minutes shy of tender.
Drain the pasta. Do not rinse it.

stepGroups.title: Finish the Chili Mac
items: Add the drained macaroni to the chili.
Simmer for 4 to 6 minutes, stirring often, until the pasta finishes cooking in the sauce and the chili mac becomes cohesive.
Stir in the apple cider vinegar ({{quantity:apple-cider-vinegar}}), lime juice ({{quantity:fresh-lime-juice}}), and cilantro or parsley ({{quantity:fresh-cilantro-or-parsley}}).
Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.
Taste and adjust with salt, vinegar, or lime juice as needed.
